stonewatch_board.text: Wolfpack Culling keeps returning to the board because the Ashwood packs still test the road whenever the watch blinks.
Merchant Recovery matters more than its coin suggests. Lose enough lockboxes and the road starts teaching bad habits.
Stonewatch's board quartermasters are prioritizing basic guard gear and ashwood hide lots this week.

merchant_guild_halls.text: The Merchant Guild likes heroes who keep the ledgers boring: safe roads, arriving cargo, and no funeral tariffs.
Stonewatch's guild halls are already repeating which names get honest work and which names only get watched.

valewood_trails.text: Alpha on Ashwood Trail pays because one disciplined beast can thin a border road faster than a gang of panicked raiders.
Valewood's scouts say the deep paths are listening harder than usual. The clans do not waste that warning.
Valewood traders are short on tracking kits and paying extra for clean beast hides.

emberfall_wards.text: Silence the Weeping Shrine if you want shrine folk to believe you can keep ward-duty when the old stone pushes back.
The ash roads only stay roads because Emberfall keeps paying people to hold a line most of the realm would rather forget.
Emberfall archivists are buying ritual salt and rare herbs faster than caravan ledgers can replace them.

ironridge_convoys.text: Nobody hauling ore through Ironridge forgets Gorgos for long. The pass remembers every cart he broke.
Ironridge convoy work is never glamorous. That is exactly why the hold judges people by whether they finish it.
Ironridge forges are posting premium rates for clean iron ore and true forge stone.

stormbreak_harbor.text: Stormbreak Harbor looks loud and ordinary until the weather shifts; then everyone learns who has kept the coast line honest.
A paid berth to Stormbreak buys order and escort, not mercy from the sea.
Stormbreak factors just unloaded rare coastal imports, and inland guilds are already bidding.

vaultreach_relics.text: Blackroot Ash Mark is the sort of contract that tells Vaultreach whether you belong near relic ledgers or outside the gate.
People in Vaultreach speak quietly about the Vault Keeper, which is how you know they still expect someone to have to face it.
Vaultreach salvage crews are bringing in ancient alloy and construct parts faster than scribes can catalog them.

bard_ballads.text: The road songs change quickly in Valerion. One good kill or one held contract can put a name into a chorus by dusk.
Bards know what merchants and clan scouts know: reputation arrives in a town earlier than the hero who earned it.
